# SLIP-39 D1 test data generation

## `slip39_vectors.json`

The official SLIP-0039 test vectors, copied VERBATIM (no hand-typing) from
`trezor/python-shamir-mnemonic`:

```
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/trezor/python-shamir-mnemonic/master/vectors.json -o /tmp/vectors.json
```

Stored as a JSON object keyed by the ORIGINAL upstream vector index (string) →
the upstream 4-tuple `[desc, [mnemonics], master_hex, xprv]`. The kept indices
are the positives `0, 3, 17, 35, 42` and negatives `1, 4, 5, 9, 12, 13`. All
valid vectors use passphrase `"TREZOR"`.

## `slip39_fixtures.json`

The 23/27/30-word intermediate-length round-trips the static official corpus
lacks (it carries only 128/256-bit). Generated by the Rust oracle
(`mnemonic-toolkit::slip39::slip39_split`) with a FIXED per-case ChaCha20Rng
seed + identifier (deterministic), via a throwaway integration test:

`crates/mnemonic-toolkit/tests/zz_slip39_fixture_gen.rs` (run from the
`mnemonic-toolkit` repo root):

```
SLIP39_FIXTURE_OUT=/tmp/slip39_fixtures.json \
  cargo test -p mnemonic-toolkit --test zz_slip39_fixture_gen -- --ignored --nocapture
cp /tmp/slip39_fixtures.json <seedhammer>/slip39/testdata/slip39_fixtures.json
```

Coverage: master-secret lengths {16,20,24,28,32} B (→ share word counts
{20,23,27,30,33}) × topologies {1-of-1, 2-of-3, group(2-of-3 over 2 groups,
group-threshold 2)}. `passphrase` is `"TREZOR"`; `extendable` is always false on
this path (ext=1 coverage is the official idx-42 vector). Each case is
`{desc, secret_hex, passphrase, mnemonics[]}`, where `mnemonics` is exactly a
recoverable threshold subset (Combine requires an exact-count set).

The generator is a TEST FIXTURE ONLY; the SLIP-39 split side is NOT ported into
the firmware (combine-only).
